avg_annual_opex_per_miu_usd.rationale: Plan names $2–3M as the baseline; Risk 4 names a 20% escalation scenario (+$400–600K/MIU) due to logistics and engineer-rotation costs, captured at the upper bound.

avg_active_fleet_size_units.rationale: Fleet ramps from 3 pilots in Year 2 to 30 units by Year 10; time-weighted average is mid-teens, with downside reflecting Phase-2 launch slip and upside reflecting accelerated scaling.

actual_uptime_share.rationale: Plan targets >90%; Risk 1 names 'uptime drops below 80%' as the dominant operational shock if cannibalization fails; base sits just below the 90% target reflecting realistic field friction.

actual_review_load_share.rationale: Issue 3 sensitivity: at AI FPR 10% the override produces ~11.5% load; at FPR 15% it reaches ~17.25%; upper tail captures the Risk 3 30–40 reviewers/MIU collapse scenario.

modular_mtbf_actual_improvement_share.rationale: Issue 1 names the 30% target as Builder's central R&D bet; downside reflects mostly-failed R&D where modular parts barely match OEM, upside reflects accelerated-life-test validation exceeding spec.

modular_mtbf_improvement_target_share.rationale: Plan commits to a 30% MTBF improvement over OEM as the Go/No-Go threshold; spread reflects how strictly the Month-18 review treats the target (stricter at the upper end).
